# UC-22.59.14 · Annual cyber-drill + tabletop after-action register — recover function MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3 §2.5 and ISM §8.2 evidence chain

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Like fire drills on a ship, the crew must also practice handling a cyber incident at least once a year, and afterwards they write down what they learned and what to fix. We watch each ship's drill log and warn the company when a drill is overdue or a lesson has not been acted on.*

---

## Description

Validates that every vessel in the fleet has performed at least one cyber-drill within the past 365 days, that the drill scenario and lessons-learned are documented, and that action items from the after-action review are closed within the SLA.

## Value

ISM emergency-preparedness drills are a known PSC inspection item; cyber drills are now in scope. Without an evidence chain, the auditor records the gap as a major non-conformity; the same finding can lead to detention at PSC inspection.

## Implementation

(1) Build the drill register KV Store. (2) Wire the company training department to log drill completion + after-action items into the register. (3) Wire ServiceNow tasks to feed action-item-closure status. (4) Schedule UC-22.59.14 weekly; route as P2 for overdue drills and P3 for action-item closure breach.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.59.14` archived weekly to `audit_evidence` with `imo_clause="IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s2-5"`. Every row pins (vessel_or_fleet, last_cyber_drill, drill_type, drill_scenario, lessons_logged, action_items_open, action_items_overdue, status).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

A vessel completed its last cyber-drill 400 days ago. UC-22.59.14 MUST flag status=overdue.

### Negative scenario

Vessel completed a tabletop 90 days ago and all action items closed. UC-22.59.14 MUST record status=current.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.59.1 and UC-22.59.2 in production so vessel + DPA registers are authoritative.
- ServiceNow ITSM task workflow for cyber-drill action items.

## Step 1 — Drill register KV Store

```ini
[imo_cyber_drill_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.vessel_or_fleet = string
field.imo_number = string
field.last_drill_completed_at = time
field.drill_type = string  # tabletop | simulation | full-exercise
field.drill_scenario = string  # ransomware-shore | ecdis-spoof | gnss-jam | propulsion-malware | satcom-mitm | etc
field.action_items_logged = number
field.action_items_open = number
field.action_items_overdue = number
```

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.14: IMO Cyber-Drill Register]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * 1
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.14,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s2-5
alert.severity = 3
```

## Step 3 — Runbooks

**`status=breach-no-drill`** (owner: CySO + DPA, severity P2): Schedule a drill within 30 days; record in the drill register on completion.

**`status=breach-actions-overdue`** (owner: CySO + Master, severity P3): Close action items within 14 days; escalate to DPA + Company Security Officer if not closed.

## Step 4 — Maritime-specific notes

- **SOLAS Chapter VII drills**: align cyber-drill scheduling with SOLAS emergency drills already in the master's schedule.
- **Mixed cyber + safety drills**: a fire-in-server-room drill exercising both cyber-response and safety-emergency response can satisfy this UC for that vessel.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup imo_cyber_drill_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_since_drill = round((now_ts - last_drill_completed_at) / 86400, 1)
| eval drill_status = case(
    days_since_drill > 365, "overdue",
    days_since_drill > 330, "due-soon",
    1=1, "current")
| eval action_overdue_count = if(isnotnull(action_items_overdue), action_items_overdue, 0)
| eval status = case(
    drill_status = "overdue", "breach-no-drill",
    drill_status = "due-soon", "warning-drill-due",
    action_overdue_count > 0, "breach-actions-overdue",
    1=1, "compliant")
| where status NOT IN ("compliant")
| sort 0 -days_since_drill
| table vessel_or_fleet imo_number last_drill_completed_at drill_type drill_scenario days_since_drill action_items_open action_items_overdue status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Fleet drill currency table (days since last drill). (Panel 2) Overdue-action histogram. (Panel 3) Drill cadence trend (last 24 months). (Panel 4) Top scenario types.

## Known False Positives

1. **New-build vessels**: a vessel under construction or delivered <30 days has no drill history; honour a 90-day onboarding grace window. 2. **Fleet-level drills**: a single SCAREX tabletop may cover multiple vessels; the drill register must enumerate every vessel attending.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_14_exceptions` with the Company Security Officer documented approval.

## References

- [IMO MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3 Rev.2 §2.5 Recover](https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/OurWork/Security/Documents/MSC-FAL.1-Circ.3-Rev.2.pdf)
- [BIMCO Guidelines on Cyber Security Onboard Ships — drills annex](https://www.bimco.org/about-us-and-our-members/publications/the-guidelines-on-cyber-security-onboard-ships)
